response.justification: While the candidate brings valuable geospatial platform experience and exceptional testing rigor, the technology gap is substantial. C++ experience is limited to 6 months over 11 years ago, with no Rust experience. Recent work spans web services and TypeScript, not systems-level programming. For a mid-senior routing infrastructure role requiring modern C++ or Rust, the ramp-up time and learning curve would be significant.
strengths: Deep geospatial platform expertise—founded and maintains GISCollective, a production geo-data system, which is a rare and directly relevant skill
Exceptional commitment to testing, code quality, and software craftsmanship—created test libraries, led TDD workshops, and applied rigorous testing practices across roles
Proven learner across diverse technical domains—web, mobile, desktop, embedded, and multiple languages, demonstrating adaptability to unfamiliar technical stacks
concerns: Minimal and outdated C++ experience—only 6 months at National Instruments (2014–2015) with no modern systems-level C++ work in the past decade
No Rust experience and recent work is web-based (TypeScript, Node.js), not systems programming, making the transition to a routing data pipeline role non-trivial
Geospatial experience is web-application-focused, not data processing pipelines or tile generation—lacks hands-on experience with the core domain of this role
